title: BitNet-TT session 4: PCC > 0.99 within 70 t/s budget
goal: Hit decode_tps >= 70 t/s AND prefill PCC > 0.99. Speed already above 70; session investigates whether the 4.25 t/s head-room is enough to buy +0.0093 PCC via layer asymmetry or non-layer precision levers.
constraints: Argmax match stays True on the single-prompt probe at every checkpoint.
All measurements on TT server via ssh TT.
Every experiment recorded with before/after decode_tps AND prefill PCC.

stories.title: Measure bf16 layer asymmetry: layer 29 alone vs layer 0 alone
acceptanceCriteria: bench + accuracy measured with BITNET_BF16_LAYERS=29 (last layer).
Results compared to the session 3 layer-0-alone data point.
Conclusion: do last-layer bf16 and first-layer bf16 contribute equally, or is one asymmetrically bigger?
stories.notes: MEASURED. layer 0 bf16: +0.00086 PCC at -2.07 t/s. layer 29 bf16: +0.00016 PCC at -2.83 t/s. Early-layer PCC gain is 5.4x the late-layer gain AND cheaper per layer. Conclusion: quantisation error seeded at layer 0 amplifies through downstream residuals, so bf16 on early layers is strictly more cost-effective than on late layers.

stories.title: Measure multi-layer bf16 combo: 0+29 and (if needed) 0+1+28+29
acceptanceCriteria: If the 2-layer combo shows supralinear PCC gain (>2x single-layer), escalate to 0+1+28+29.
Otherwise record the result as linear and move on.
stories.notes: MEASURED with 0+1 (both first two layers, higher expected yield than 0+29 given US-301 asymmetry). Result: +0.00063 PCC at -3.55 t/s, which is LESS PCC than layer 0 alone. Layer-to-layer interactions are not simply additive at this scale and stay within measurement noise. Supralinear escalation is not justified. Full table in docs/session_4_pcc_ceiling.md.

stories.title: Test HiFi4 matmul compute config for ternary_matmul decode path
acceptanceCriteria: The per-step decode matmul kernel_config is switchable between HiFi2 and HiFi4 via a flag (BITNET_DECODE_MATMUL_FIDELITY).
If HiFi4 clears PCC >= 0.99 within the 70 t/s speed budget, set as default.
stories.notes: MEASURED. HiFi4 decode path: decode_tps 73.84 (-0.41), PCC 0.980679 (bit-identical to HiFi2 baseline). The env flag only controls the non-ternary decode matmul (primarily the lm_head chunks) — those already run on bfp8 weights, so HiFi4 has nothing more to accumulate. ternary_matmul's internal math fidelity is not reachable from Python and would need a tt-metal kernel edit. Default stays HiFi2; env override kept for future kernel-level sweeps.

stories.title: Combine best levers to hit 70 t/s + PCC > 0.99 (or document the ceiling)
acceptanceCriteria: Final config measured on 128 tok + 32 step accuracy.
If target met: README + TODO updated, new baseline committed.
If target missed: exact achieved (decode_tps, PCC) pair recorded in docs/ with the specific blocker.
stories.notes: TARGET MISSED (PCC axis). Best measured PCC above the 70 t/s line is 0.981537 (bf16 @ layer 0, decode_tps 72.18). That is a +0.00086 improvement on baseline, well short of the +0.0093 needed for PCC > 0.99. Exact ceiling written to docs/session_4_pcc_ceiling.md. Root cause of the ceiling: activation INT8 re-quantisation inside the ternary_matmul kernel is the dominant error source; swapping a small number of weight matrices to bf16 does not recover enough because the activation side re-quantises at every projection. Current HEAD remains at baseline config (74.25 t/s / 0.9807).
